functions.description: Linearly interpolates between two values.

functions.name: remap
functions.description: Remaps a value from one range to another.

functions.name: clamp
functions.description: Clamps a value to a range. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: sign
functions.description: Returns the sign of a value. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: smoothstep
functions.description: Applies smoothstep interpolation between two edges.

functions.name: inverseLerp
functions.description: Returns the interpolation factor of a value between two bounds.

functions.name: hslToRgb
functions.description: Converts HSL color values to RGBA channels.

functions.name: fromHex
functions.description: Converts a hex color string to RGBA channels.

functions.name: rgbToHsl
functions.description: Converts RGB channels to HSL values.

functions.name: rectUnion
functions.description: Returns the union rectangle for two rectangles.

functions.name: rectFromCenter
functions.description: Creates a rectangle tuple from center coordinates and size.

functions.name: polygonClip
functions.description: Clips a flat polygon point table against a plane.

functions.name: aabbTree
functions.description: Creates an empty AABB tree. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: newCircle
functions.description: Creates a circle primitive. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: polygonIntersection
functions.description: Returns polygon intersection points for two polygon tables.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First polygon table of `{x, y}` points.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second polygon table of `{x, y}` points.

functions.name: polygonUnion
functions.description: Returns polygon union points for two polygon tables.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First polygon table of `{x, y}` points.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second polygon table of `{x, y}` points.

functions.name: polygonDifference
functions.description: Returns polygon difference points for two polygon tables.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: First polygon table of `{x, y}` points.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Second polygon table of `{x, y}` points.

functions.name: voronoi
functions.description: Builds Voronoi cells from a polygon-style point table.

modules.name: minimap
modules.description: `lurek.minimap` -- Lua bindings for grid minimaps, terrain colors, fog, object markers, overlays, layers, hover conversion, and render command output.

functions.name: newMinimap
functions.description: Creates a minimap with grid dimensions and optional display size.

parameters.name: display_w
parameters.description: Optional display width in pixels, defaulting to 200.

parameters.name: display_h
parameters.description: Optional display height in pixels, defaulting to 200.

modules.name: mods
modules.description: `lurek.mods` -- Lua bindings for mod metadata, mod manager load order/dependency helpers, content registries, and API version checks.

functions.name: newMod
functions.description: Creates a mod metadata handle from a Lua table.

functions.name: newModManager
functions.description: Creates an empty mod manager. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: newRegistry
functions.description: Creates an empty content registry.

functions.name: checkApiVersion
functions.description: Checks whether a mod API version is compatible with a host version.

modules.name: network
modules.description: `lurek.network` -- Lua bindings for ENet-style hosts, async network runtime, message packing, lobby helpers, relay tickets, and snapshot prediction.

functions.name: newHost
functions.description: Creates a network host from an options table.

functions.name: newServer
functions.description: Creates a server host from an options table.

functions.name: newClient
functions.description: Creates a client host and connects to an address.

functions.name: newRuntime
functions.description: Creates a background network runtime.

functions.name: pack
functions.description: Packs a supported Lua value into a binary network message string.

functions.name: unpack
functions.description: Unpacks a binary network message string into Lua values.

functions.name: createLobby
functions.description: Broadcasts lobby information and returns it as a table.

parameters.name: player_count
parameters.description: Optional current player count, defaulting to 1.

functions.name: discoverLobbies
functions.description: Discovers broadcast lobbies. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

parameters.name: timeout_ms
parameters.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaulting to 500.

functions.name: createRoom
functions.description: Creates a local room record. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: listRooms
functions.description: Lists known local room records. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: joinRoom
functions.description: Joins a room by id when available. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: leaveRoom
functions.description: Leaves a room by id when available. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: syncEntity
functions.description: Broadcasts a packed entity sync payload through a network host.

parameters.name: reliable
parameters.description: Optional reliable flag, defaulting to false.

functions.name: newRelayTicket
functions.description: Creates an encoded relay ticket. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: parseRelayTicket
functions.description: Parses an encoded relay ticket. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: makePunchProbe
functions.description: Creates a relay punch probe payload for a peer id.

functions.name: parsePunchProbe
functions.description: Parses a relay punch probe payload.

functions.name: predictLinear
functions.description: Predicts an entity snapshot forward by linear velocity.

functions.name: reconcileSnapshot
functions.description: Reconciles a predicted snapshot toward an authoritative snapshot.

modules.name: parallax
modules.description: `lurek.parallax` -- Lua bindings for parallax layers, parallax sets, presets, automatic camera rendering, tiling, blend modes, and effect chains.

functions.name: newLayer
functions.description: Creates a parallax layer from an options table.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options table with required `texture` and optional scrolling, repeat, z, opacity, tint, blend, visibility, scale, tiling, depth, tile size, motion stretch, and effects fields.

functions.name: newSet
functions.description: Creates an empty parallax layer set.

functions.name: newPresetLayer
functions.description: Creates a parallax layer from a named preset and texture image.

modules.name: particle
modules.description: `lurek.particle` -- Lua bindings for particle systems, trails, presets, TOML configs, physics collision, custom emission callbacks, death callbacks, and module-level forwarding helpers.

functions.name: newSystem
functions.description: Creates a particle system from an optional config table.

functions.name: newTrail
functions.description: Creates a trail effect. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: fromTOML
functions.description: Creates a particle system from a TOML config file.

functions.name: newPreset
functions.description: Creates a particle system from a named preset.

modules.name: pathfind
modules.description: `lurek.pathfind` - Lua bindings for navigation grids, unit pathfinding, flow fields, path grids, hex grids, JPS grids, nav meshes, range maps, and tilemap-derived path data.

functions.name: newNavGrid
functions.description: Creates a navigation grid. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: newPathfinder
functions.description: Creates a unit pathfinder for a navigation grid.

functions.name: newFlowField
functions.description: Creates a flow field for a navigation grid.

functions.name: newPathGrid
functions.description: Creates a cell-size path grid. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: newPathFlowField
functions.description: Creates an AI flow field from a path grid.

functions.name: setThreadCount
functions.description: Records a warning because pathfinding thread count is not implemented.

functions.name: getThreadCount
functions.description: Returns the pathfinding thread count.

functions.name: newNavGridFromTileMap
functions.description: Creates a navigation grid from a tilemap layer and blocked gid table.

functions.name: newHexGrid
functions.description: Creates a hex grid. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: newJpsGrid
functions.description: Creates a Jump Point Search grid. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: newNavMesh
functions.description: Creates an empty navigation mesh. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: rangeMap
functions.description: Computes reachable cells from range map options.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options with dimensions, origin, budget, optional diagonal flag, costs, and blocked cells.

modules.name: patterns
modules.description: `lurek.patterns` — Design pattern utilities: event buses, object pools, state machines, command stacks, observers, mediators, factories, data structures, behavior trees, and graphs.

functions.name: newEventBus
functions.description: Create a new publish/subscribe event bus for decoupled communication between game systems.

functions.name: newObjectPool
functions.description: Create a new object pool for reusing pre-allocated game objects to reduce allocation overhead.

functions.name: newCommandStack
functions.description: Create a new undo/redo command stack for recording and reversing player or editor actions.

functions.name: newServiceLocator
functions.description: Create a new service locator for registering and retrieving shared services by name at runtime.

functions.name: newFactory
functions.description: Create a new factory for producing typed game objects from registered constructor functions.

functions.name: newSimpleState
functions.description: Create a new finite state machine with enter/exit/update callbacks per state.

functions.name: newBlackboard
functions.description: Create a new shared key-value blackboard supporting reactive watchers for game logic variables.

functions.name: newObserver
functions.description: Create a new reactive observer that stores values and notifies subscribers when they change.

functions.name: newThrottle
functions.description: Create a new throttle that limits how often an action can fire, enforcing a minimum interval.

functions.name: newDebounce
functions.description: Create a new debounce that delays firing until input stops for a specified wait period.

functions.name: newPriorityQueue
functions.description: Create a new priority queue that orders elements by numeric priority (highest first).

functions.name: newRing
functions.description: Create a new fixed-size ring buffer for numeric or string values. Oldest entries are overwritten when full.

parameters.name: capacity
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries the ring can hold.

functions.name: newFunnel
functions.description: Create a new batching funnel that collects events over a time window and flushes them together.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Time window in seconds before auto-flush.

parameters.name: maxEntries
parameters.description: Maximum entries before forced flush (0 = no limit).

functions.name: newRelationshipManager
functions.description: Create a new relationship manager for tracking numeric values and named levels between entity pairs.

functions.name: newMediator
functions.description: Create a new mediator for channel-based message passing between decoupled game systems.

functions.name: newStrategy
functions.description: Create a new strategy pattern container for hot-swappable algorithm implementations.

functions.name: newStack
functions.description: Create a new LIFO stack with optional capacity limit.

functions.name: newQueue
functions.description: Create a new FIFO queue with optional capacity limit.

functions.name: newList
functions.description: Create a new dynamic array list with indexed access, insertion, removal, and search.

functions.name: newSet
functions.description: Create a new string set with add/remove/has operations and set algebra (union, intersection).

functions.name: newMap
functions.description: Create a new string-keyed dictionary (map) with keys/values/entries access and merge support.

functions.name: newWeightedRandom
functions.description: Create a new weighted random selection pool. Add items with weights and pick random selections.

functions.name: newBehaviorTree
functions.description: Create a new behavior tree for AI decision-making with sequences, selectors, parallels, and leaf actions.

functions.name: newGraph
functions.description: Create a new graph data structure with directed or undirected edges, BFS, DFS, and connectivity queries.

parameters.name: undirected
parameters.description: If true, edges are bidirectional (default false).

modules.name: physics
modules.description: `lurek.physics` — 2D rigid-body physics: worlds, bodies, shapes, joints, raycasting, collision queries, terrain, cellular simulation, and debug drawing via Rapier2D.

functions.name: newWorld
functions.description: Creates a new physics world with the given gravity vector.

functions.name: step
functions.description: Steps a physics world forward by dt seconds (free-function variant).

functions.name: destroyWorld
functions.description: No-op placeholder for API parity. Worlds are freed when no longer referenced.

functions.name: newBody
functions.description: Creates a new body in a world (free-function variant).

functions.name: getBody
functions.description: Returns position and velocity of a body (free-function variant for quick queries).

functions.name: setBodyVelocity
functions.description: Sets a body's velocity (free-function variant).

functions.name: isSleepingAllowed
functions.description: Checks if sleeping is allowed on a body (free-function variant).

functions.name: setSleepingAllowed
functions.description: Sets whether a body is allowed to sleep (free-function variant).

functions.name: newRectangleShape
functions.description: Creates a rectangle collision shape with the given dimensions.

functions.name: newCircleShape
functions.description: Creates a circle collision shape with the given radius.

functions.name: newEdgeShape
functions.description: Creates an edge (line segment) collision shape between two local points.

functions.name: newPolygonShape
functions.description: Creates a convex polygon collision shape from vertex coordinate pairs.

functions.name: newChainShape
functions.description: Creates a chain (polyline) collision shape. Useful for terrain outlines.

parameters.name: closed
parameters.description: If true, connects last vertex to first.

functions.name: attachShape
functions.description: Attaches a previously created shape to a body, using the shape's stored material properties.

functions.name: getCollisions
functions.description: Returns all collision events from the last world step as {body_a, body_b} pairs.

functions.name: debugDraw
functions.description: Enables or disables automatic physics debug overlay rendering for the next frame.

functions.name: drawDebugGpu
functions.description: Queues a GPU-rendered physics debug visualization using the world's current body state.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Optional config: {bodyColor, staticColor, sleepColor, sensorColor, lineWidth}.

functions.name: newTerrain
functions.description: Creates a destructible terrain grid linked to a physics world for automatic collider generation.

parameters.name: world
parameters.description: The physics world that will own the generated colliders.

functions.name: newCellular
functions.description: Creates a new cellular automaton simulation grid for particle-like physics (sand, water, fire).

functions.name: testAABB
functions.signature: (ax, ay, aw, ah, bx, by, bw, bh)
functions.description: Tests whether two axis-aligned bounding boxes overlap. Lightweight collision check without physics world.

functions.name: testCircles
functions.description: Tests whether two circles overlap. Lightweight collision check without physics world.

functions.name: testPoint
functions.description: Tests whether a point lies inside an AABB. Lightweight check without physics world.

functions.name: testCircleAABB
functions.signature: (cx, cy, cr, ax, ay, aw, ah)
functions.description: Tests whether a circle overlaps an AABB. Lightweight check without physics world.

modules.name: pipeline
modules.description: `lurek.pipeline` — Declarative task pipelines with dependency ordering, retry logic, branching, and async coroutine execution.

functions.name: newStep
functions.description: Creates a new pipeline step with the given name and an optional callback function.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback executed when this step runs.

functions.name: newPipeline
functions.description: Creates a new empty pipeline with an optional name. Add steps via addStep() or addConditional().

functions.name: fromTable
functions.description: Creates a pipeline pre-populated with steps from a declarative table definition. Each step entry can specify name, deps, delay, optional, retryCount, retryDelay, async, tag, and fn.

parameters.name: definition
parameters.description: A table with optional name, errorMode, and a steps array.

modules.name: procgen
modules.description: `lurek.procgen` — Procedural generation tools: noise, dungeon generators, wave function collapse, heightmaps, L-systems, name generation, voronoi, biomes, and world graphs.

functions.name: cellularAutomata
functions.description: Generate a cave or organic map using cellular automata rules.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: fill (0.0–1.0 initial fill ratio), iterations, birth threshold, survive threshold, seed.

functions.name: floodFill
functions.description: Flood-fill a grid from a starting cell, marking all connected cells that pass a threshold test.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Flat array of u8 cell values (length = width*height).

parameters.name: above
parameters.description: If true, fill cells >= threshold; if false (default), fill cells < threshold.

functions.name: perlinNoise
functions.description: Sample periodic 2D Perlin noise at a given coordinate.

functions.name: poissonDisk
functions.description: Generate evenly-spaced random points using Poisson disk sampling. Useful for placing trees, NPCs, or loot without clustering.

parameters.name: minDist
parameters.description: Minimum distance between any two points.

parameters.name: maxAttempts
parameters.description: Rejection attempts per active point (default 30). Higher = denser fill.

functions.name: voronoi
functions.description: Compute a Voronoi diagram from a set of seed points. Returns region ownership, distance-to-nearest, and distance-to-second-nearest for each cell.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: warp_scale, warp_strength, seed for domain warping.

functions.name: bspDungeon
functions.description: Generate a dungeon layout using Binary Space Partitioning. Produces non-overlapping rooms connected by corridors.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: width, height, min_size (minimum leaf size), max_depth (BSP tree depth), seed, padding.

functions.name: bspDungeonWithPrefabs
functions.description: Generate a BSP dungeon and stamp named prefab rooms into suitable leaves. Returns dungeon layout plus prefab placement info.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: BSP options: width, height, min_size, max_depth, seed, padding.

parameters.name: prefabs
parameters.description: Array of prefab definitions: {name, width, height}.

functions.name: roomsDungeon
functions.description: Generate a dungeon by placing random non-overlapping rooms and connecting them with corridors. Also returns a full tile grid.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: width, height, max_rooms, min_room_size, max_room_size, seed.

functions.name: roomsDungeonWithPrefabs
functions.description: Generate a rooms-based dungeon and place named prefabs into qualifying rooms. Prefabs can have custom shape masks.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Room generation options: width, height, max_rooms, min_room_size, max_room_size, seed.

parameters.name: prefabs
parameters.description: Array of prefab definitions: {name, width, height, mask (optional flat u8[])}.

parameters.name: stampValue
parameters.description: Tile value written for prefab cells in the grid (default 3).

functions.name: heightmap
functions.description: Generate a fractal heightmap using multi-octave noise with optional hydraulic erosion.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: width, height, scale, octaves, lacunarity, persistence, seed, erosion_passes.

functions.name: heightmapFromCellular
functions.description: Convert a cellular automata grid into a heightmap by distance-transforming the floor cells.

parameters.name: floorValue
parameters.description: Cell value treated as open floor (default 0).

functions.name: wfcGenerate
functions.description: Run Wave Function Collapse to generate a grid of tile IDs satisfying adjacency constraints.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: width, height, seed, max_attempts, tiles (array of {id, weight}), adjacencies (map of tile_id -> allowed neighbor IDs[]).

functions.name: lsystem
functions.description: Expand an L-system grammar and return the resulting string. Useful for generating branching structures like trees, rivers, or cave networks.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: axiom (starting string), iterations (expansion count), rules (table mapping single-char keys to replacement strings).

functions.name: lsystemSegments
functions.description: Expand an L-system and interpret the result as turtle-graphics commands, returning line segments.

functions.name: generateName
functions.description: Generate a single random name based on a Markov chain trained from sample names. Great for NPC names, place names, or item names.

parameters.name: samples
parameters.description: Array of example name strings to learn from.

functions.name: generateNames
functions.description: Generate multiple random names in one call using Markov chains trained from sample data.

parameters.name: samples
parameters.description: Array of example name strings to learn from.

functions.name: worldGraph
functions.description: Generate a connected world graph with named regions and weighted edges. Useful for overworld maps, trade routes, or quest connectivity.

functions.name: noiseMap
functions.description: Generate a 2D noise map with configurable scale, octaves, and offsets. Runs on a single thread.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: scale_x, scale_y, octaves, lacunarity, persistence, offset_x, offset_y, seed.

functions.name: noiseMapParallel
functions.description: Generate a 2D noise map using multiple threads for faster computation on large maps. Uses seed 0.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: scale_x, scale_y, octaves, lacunarity, persistence, offset_x, offset_y.

functions.name: noiseMapParallelSeeded
functions.description: Generate a 2D noise map using multiple threads with a specific seed for reproducible results.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options: scale_x, scale_y, octaves, lacunarity, persistence, offset_x, offset_y, seed.

functions.name: simplex2d
functions.description: Sample 2D simplex noise at a point. Returns a value roughly in [-1, 1].

functions.name: simplex3d
functions.description: Sample 3D simplex noise at a point. The third axis can be used for animation or layering.

parameters.name: z
parameters.description: Z coordinate (often time or layer index).

functions.name: newBiomeClassifier
functions.description: Create a BiomeClassifier object with custom threshold rules for mapping height/moisture/temperature to biome types.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Optional rules: ocean_threshold, coast_threshold, mountain_threshold, ice_cap_threshold, cold_temperature, warm_temperature, dry_moisture, wet_moisture.

functions.name: biomeColor
functions.description: Get the default RGBA display color for a biome type name. Useful for minimap or debug visualization.

modules.name: province
modules.description: `lurek.province` — Province-based strategic map system with grid regions, borders, ownership tracking, adjacency queries, and map-mode rendering.

functions.name: newFromPng
functions.description: Creates a new province registry by loading a color-coded PNG where each unique color represents a distinct province. The PNG is parsed into a grid and adjacencies are computed automatically.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique registry name for later retrieval.

parameters.name: png_path
parameters.description: Path to the province map PNG (relative to game directory or absolute).

functions.name: sanitizeMarkedPng
functions.description: Pre-processes a marker PNG by replacing capital and label marker pixels with the surrounding province color. Outputs a cleaned PNG suitable for `newFromPng`. Returns a summary of pixel replacements.

parameters.name: input_png
parameters.description: Path to the source marker PNG.

parameters.name: output_png
parameters.description: Path to write the sanitized output PNG.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Marker detection thresholds: capital_min (number?), label_r_min (number?), label_g_max (number?), label_b_min (number?), search_radius (number?).

functions.name: get
functions.description: Retrieves an existing province registry by name. Returns nil if no registry with that name has been created.

functions.name: exists
functions.description: Checks whether a province registry with the given name exists.

functions.name: remove
functions.description: Removes a province registry by name and clears the active registry if it was the one removed. Returns true if a registry was actually removed.

functions.name: setActive
functions.description: Sets the named registry as the active province registry. Returns false if no registry with that name exists.

functions.name: getActive
functions.description: Returns the currently active province registry, or nil if none is set.

functions.name: zoomCameraAt
functions.signature: lurek.province.zoomCameraAt(anchor_x, anchor_y, cam_x, cam_y, old_zoom, new_zoom)
functions.description: Computes new camera position after zooming centered on an anchor point. Keeps the anchor point visually stationary on screen while the zoom level changes.

modules.name: raycaster
modules.description: `lurek.raycaster` - Provides a pseudo-3D raycasting engine for first-person dungeon crawlers with textured walls, floors, and ceilings.

functions.name: new
functions.description: Creates a new raycaster map with the given grid dimensions.

functions.name: newMap
functions.description: Creates a new raycaster map (alias for `new`).

functions.name: projectColumn
functions.description: Computes the projected wall-column height for a given distance, FOV, and screen height.

functions.name: distanceShade
functions.description: Returns a brightness multiplier (0.0..1.0) based on distance for fog/darkness falloff.

parameters.name: maxDistance
parameters.description: Distance at which shade reaches zero.

functions.name: newDoorManager
functions.description: Creates a new door manager for tracking and animating sliding doors.

functions.name: newHeightMap
functions.description: Creates a new height map for variable floor/ceiling heights across the grid.

functions.name: newPointLight
functions.signature: (x, y, r, g, b, radius, intensity)
functions.description: Creates a new point light with position, color, radius, and intensity.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: Light falloff radius in world units.

functions.name: newSpriteManager
functions.description: Creates a new sprite manager for tracking and projecting billboard sprites.

modules.name: render
modules.description: `lurek.render` - Provides 2D drawing primitives, texture rendering, text output, blend modes, and render state management.

functions.name: setColor
functions.description: Sets the active drawing color for all subsequent draw operations.

functions.name: getColor
functions.description: Returns the current drawing color.

functions.name: setBackgroundColor
functions.description: Sets the background clear color used at the start of each frame.

functions.name: getBackgroundColor
functions.description: Returns the current background clear color.

functions.name: rectangle
functions.description: Draws a rectangle. If rx is provided, draws a rounded rectangle.

parameters.name: rx
parameters.description: Horizontal corner radius for rounded rectangle.

functions.name: circle
functions.description: Draws a circle. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: ellipse
functions.description: Draws an ellipse. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: triangle
functions.signature: (mode, x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3)
functions.description: Draws a triangle from three vertex positions.

functions.name: line
functions.description: Draws a line between two points, or a polyline through multiple points.

parameters.description: Coordinate values: x1, y1, x2, y2 for a line, or more for a polyline.

functions.name: polygon
functions.description: Draws a polygon from a flat list of x,y vertex coordinates.

parameters.description: Flat vertex coordinates: x1, y1, x2, y2, ... (minimum 3 vertices).

functions.name: arc
functions.signature: lurek.render.arc(mode, x, y, radius, angle1, angle2, [segments])
functions.description: Draws a circular arc. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: points
functions.description: Draws one or more points. Accepts either a table of {x,y} pairs or flat x,y coordinate values.

parameters.description: Point data as a table of {x,y} sub-tables, or flat x1,y1,x2,y2,... values.

functions.name: draw
functions.description: Draws a drawable object (Image, Canvas, SpriteBatch, or Mesh) at the given position with optional transform.

functions.name: drawq
functions.description: Draws a sub-region of an image defined by a Quad, with optional transform.

parameters.name: quad
parameters.description: Quad defining the source rectangle within the image.

functions.name: drawMany
functions.description: Batch-draws multiple images in one call. Each entry is a table: {image, x, y, r, sx, sy, ox, oy}.

functions.name: printRotated
functions.description: Draws text centered and rotated around its midpoint.

functions.name: print
functions.description: Draws text using the active font at the given position.

functions.name: printf
functions.description: Draws word-wrapped and aligned text within a pixel-width limit.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum line width in pixels for wrapping.

functions.name: printRich
functions.description: Draws rich text composed of individually styled spans at the given position.

parameters.name: spans
parameters.description: Array of span tables, each with fields: text, r, g, b, a, scale.

functions.name: clear
functions.description: Clears all queued render commands for the current frame.

functions.name: setLineWidth
functions.description: Sets the line width for subsequent line-mode draw calls.

functions.name: getLineWidth
functions.description: Returns the current line width. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: setPointSize
functions.description: Sets the point size for subsequent point draw calls.

functions.name: getPointSize
functions.description: Returns the current point size. This function is exposed to Lua scripts.

functions.name: setBlendMode
functions.description: Sets the blend mode for subsequent draw operations.

functions.name: getBlendMode
functions.description: Returns the current blend mode name.

functions.name: newFont
functions.description: Creates a new bitmap font from a PNG sprite sheet path or returns a built-in font by pixel height.

functions.name: setFont
functions.description: Sets the active font used by print, printf, and other text rendering calls.

functions.name: getFont
functions.description: Returns the currently active font, or nil if none is set.

functions.name: getFontSizes
functions.description: Returns all available built-in font pixel heights.

functions.name: getDefaultFont
functions.description: Returns a built-in default font at the nearest available pixel height.

functions.name: getFontCellWidth
functions.description: Returns the fixed cell width of a bitmap font.

functions.name: getFontWidth
functions.description: Measures the pixel width of text using the given font.

functions.name: getFontHeight
functions.description: Returns the line height of the given font.

functions.name: getFontLineHeight
functions.description: Returns the line spacing of the given font.

functions.name: setFontLineHeight
functions.description: Sets the line height override for a font (currently a no-op stub).

functions.name: getFontAscent
functions.description: Returns the ascent (pixels above baseline) of the given font.

functions.name: getFontDescent
functions.description: Returns the descent (pixels below baseline) of the given font.

functions.name: getFontWrap
functions.description: Word-wraps text using the active font and returns the resulting lines and widest line width.

functions.name: newImage
functions.description: Loads a texture from a file path or creates one from an ImageData object.

functions.name: newCanvas
functions.description: Creates a new off-screen render target with the given dimensions.

functions.name: setCanvas
functions.description: Redirects all subsequent drawing to the given canvas. Pass nil to draw to the screen again.

parameters.name: canvas
parameters.description: Canvas to draw to, or nil for the main screen.

functions.name: getCanvas
functions.description: Returns the currently active canvas, or nil if drawing to the screen.

functions.name: getCanvasSize
functions.description: Returns the pixel dimensions of a canvas.

functions.name: newSpriteBatch
functions.description: Creates a batched sprite renderer for efficiently drawing many copies of the same texture.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: Source texture for all sprites in the batch.

functions.name: newMesh
functions.description: Creates a custom vertex mesh from an array of vertex data tables.

parameters.name: verts
parameters.description: Array of vertex tables: {{x, y, u, v, r, g, b, a}, ...}.

functions.name: newShader
functions.description: Compiles a WGSL shader program from source code and returns a handle.

functions.name: setShader
functions.description: Activates a shader for subsequent draw calls. Pass nil to restore the default shader.

parameters.name: shader
parameters.description: Shader handle to activate, or nil for default.

functions.name: getShader
functions.description: Returns the currently active shader, or nil if using the default.

functions.name: newQuad
functions.description: Creates a Quad defining a rectangular sub-region of a texture for sprite-sheet rendering.

functions.name: push
functions.description: Pushes the current transformation matrix onto the transform stack.

functions.name: pop
functions.description: Pops the top transformation matrix from the transform stack, restoring the previous one.

functions.name: translate
functions.description: Applies a translation to the current transformation matrix.

functions.name: rotate
functions.description: Applies a rotation to the current transformation matrix.

functions.name: scale
functions.description: Applies scaling to the current transformation matrix.

parameters.name: sy
parameters.description: Vertical scale factor (defaults to sx for uniform scaling).

functions.name: shear
functions.description: Applies a shear (skew) to the current transformation matrix.

functions.name: origin
functions.description: Resets the current transformation matrix to the identity (no transform).

functions.name: applyTransform
functions.description: Multiplies the current transformation matrix by a 3x3 matrix (9 values in row-major order).

parameters.name: mat
parameters.description: Flat table of 9 numbers representing a 3x3 transform matrix.

functions.name: setScissor
functions.description: Sets or clears the scissor rectangle. Only pixels inside this region are drawn. Call with no args to clear.

functions.name: getScissor
functions.description: Returns the current scissor rectangle, or nothing if no scissor is set.

functions.name: intersectScissor
functions.description: Intersects the given rectangle with the current scissor, narrowing the drawable region.

functions.name: setColorMask
functions.description: Sets which color channels are written during draw calls. Call with no args to enable all.

functions.name: getColorMask
functions.description: Returns the current color write mask.

functions.name: setWireframe
functions.description: Enables or disables wireframe rendering mode.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: True for wireframe, false for solid.

functions.name: isWireframe
functions.description: Returns whether wireframe rendering is currently active.

functions.name: stencil
functions.description: Begins a stencil write pass with the given action and reference value.

functions.name: setStencilTest
functions.description: Configures the stencil comparison test for subsequent draws. Pass nil to disable.

functions.name: setStencilMode
functions.description: Sets the stencil write action, compare function, and reference value at once.

functions.name: getStencilMode
functions.description: Returns the current stencil action, compare mode, and reference value.

functions.name: clearStencil
functions.description: Resets the stencil state to defaults (no stencil operations).

functions.name: setDepthMode
functions.description: Sets the depth comparison mode and whether depth writes are enabled.

functions.name: getDepthMode
functions.description: Returns the current depth comparison mode and write-enable flag.

functions.name: getWidth
functions.description: Returns the current window width in pixels.

functions.name: getHeight
functions.description: Returns the current window height in pixels.

functions.name: getDimensions
functions.description: Returns the current window width and height.

functions.name: setDefaultFilter
functions.description: Sets the default texture filtering mode for newly created images.

functions.name: getDefaultFilter
functions.description: Returns the current default texture filtering settings.

functions.name: getStats
functions.description: Returns a table of rendering statistics for the current frame.

functions.name: saveScreenshot
functions.description: Saves a screenshot of the current frame to a file under the save/ directory.

functions.name: captureScreenshot
functions.description: Captures a screenshot as ImageData and passes it to a callback (stub: returns 1x1 placeholder).

functions.name: newNineSlice
functions.description: Creates a 9-slice definition from an image and four border insets for scalable UI rendering.

functions.name: drawNineSlice
functions.description: Draws a 9-slice image stretched to fill the given rectangle, keeping borders unscaled.

functions.name: newShape
functions.description: Creates a new retained compound shape for accumulating draw commands.

functions.name: newDrawLayer
functions.description: Creates a new z-ordered draw layer for sorting draw callbacks by depth.

functions.name: drawQuadBezier
functions.description: Draws a quadratic Bezier curve through start, control, and end points.

functions.name: drawCubicBezier
functions.signature: lurek.render.drawCubicBezier(x1, y1, cx1, cy1, cx2, cy2, x2, y2, [segs])
functions.description: Draws a cubic Bezier curve through start, two control points, and end.

functions.name: drawPath
functions.description: Draws a vector path composed of moveTo, lineTo, quadTo, and cubicTo segments.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Array of segment tables, each with a "type" field and coordinates.

parameters.name: close
parameters.description: Close the path back to start (default false).

parameters.name: colors

modules.name: save
modules.description: `lurek.save` — Persistent game save/load system with named slots, schema versioning, auto-save, compression, and migration support.

functions.name: newSaveManager
functions.description: Create a new SaveManager instance for managing persistent game saves.

modules.name: scene
modules.description: `lurek.scene` — Stack-based scene management with animated transitions, overlay support, shared data passing, lifecycle callbacks (enter/leave/pause/resume/ready/update/draw/render), and depth-sorted rendering via `LDepthSorter`.

functions.name: push
functions.description: Push a new scene onto the stack, making it the active scene. The previously-active scene receives its `pause()` lifecycle callback and the new scene receives `enter(self, params)`. An optional visual transition (fade, slide, iris, etc.) animates between the two scenes over the specified duration.

parameters.name: scene
parameters.description: A scene table with lifecycle methods (`enter`, `leave`, `pause`, `resume`, `update`, `draw`, etc.).

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Transition animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0 (instant).

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Arbitrary data forwarded to the new scene's `enter(self, params)` callback for initialization.

functions.name: pop
functions.description: Pop the top scene off the stack and return to the previous one. The popped scene receives `leave()` and the revealed scene receives `resume()` (unless the popped scene was an overlay, in which case the underlying scene was never paused). Use this for "back" navigation, closing menus, or exiting sub-screens.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Transition animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.

functions.name: switchTo
functions.description: Replace the current top scene with a different one without changing stack depth. The old scene receives `leave()` and the new scene receives `enter(self, params)`. Unlike `push`, no scene is added to the stack — the old scene is removed and the new one takes its slot. Ideal for transitioning between peer-level game states (e.g. level 1 → level 2).

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Transition animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Arbitrary data forwarded to the new scene's `enter(self, params)` callback.

functions.name: clear
functions.description: Remove all scenes from the stack. Each removed scene receives its `leave()` callback in stack order. After this call the stack is empty and `isEmpty()` returns true. Useful for returning to a title screen or tearing down the entire scene graph.

functions.name: popTo
functions.description: Pop scenes off the stack until the named registered scene is on top. Every popped scene receives `leave()` and the target scene receives `resume()`. The target scene must have been previously added via `registerScene`. Returns false if no scene with that name exists on the stack.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The registered name of the target scene to unwind to.

functions.name: update
functions.description: Advance any active transition animation and call `update(self, dt)` on the current top scene. Call this once per frame from your main loop to drive scene logic and transition timing.

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: Delta time in seconds since the last frame (e.g. from `lurek.timer.getDelta()`).

functions.name: process
functions.description: Call `ready(self)` once on newly-pushed scenes, then call `process(self, dt)` on every active scene ordered by layer (lowest first). Use this for deterministic game-logic ticks at a fixed time step. Scenes pushed as overlays and underlying scenes all receive this callback.

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: Fixed time-step delta in seconds (e.g. 1/60 for 60-tick logic).

functions.name: processPhysics
functions.description: Call `process_physics(self, dt)` on every active scene ordered by layer. Run this callback after your physics world step so scenes can react to collision results, apply forces, or synchronize sprite positions with physics bodies.

functions.name: processLate
functions.description: Call `process_late(self, dt)` on every active scene after all other processing. Ideal for camera follow logic, HUD synchronization, deferred cleanup, or any work that depends on the final positions of game objects.

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: Delta time in seconds (same value passed to `process`).

functions.name: draw
functions.description: Call `draw(self)` on every scene in the stack from bottom to top. This is the legacy draw callback — prefer `render` and `renderUi` for world-space and screen-space separation.

functions.name: render
functions.description: Call `render(self)` on every scene in the stack from bottom to top. This is the preferred world-space rendering callback — draw sprites, tilemaps, particles, and other in-world visuals here. Runs before `renderUi`.

functions.name: renderUi
functions.description: Call `render_ui(self)` on every scene in the stack from bottom to top. Use this for screen-space HUD elements, health bars, score displays, menus, and overlays that should draw on top of the world after `render`.

functions.name: getStackSize
functions.description: Returns the total number of scenes currently on the stack, including overlays. Useful for asserting expected navigation depth or debugging scene flow.

functions.name: depth
functions.description: Alias for `getStackSize`. Returns the total number of scenes currently on the stack.

functions.name: isEmpty
functions.description: Returns true if the scene stack contains no scenes at all. Useful for guarding against calling `pop` on an empty stack or for detecting when the game should quit.

functions.name: getCurrent
functions.description: Returns the scene table currently on top of the stack, or nil if the stack is empty. Use this to inspect or call methods on the active scene directly.

functions.name: setCurrentLayer
functions.description: Set the rendering layer of the current top scene. Scenes with higher layer values are processed and drawn after lower-layer scenes. Use layers to control draw order when multiple scenes are active (e.g. game world at layer 0, HUD overlay at layer 10).

parameters.name: layer
parameters.description: Integer layer value to assign (higher = drawn later / on top).

functions.name: getCurrentLayer
functions.description: Get the rendering layer of the current top scene. Returns 0 if the stack is empty or if no layer was explicitly set.

functions.name: isTransitioning
functions.description: Returns true if a scene transition animation is currently playing. Use this to block input or skip certain logic during transitions.

functions.name: getTransitionProgress
functions.description: Returns the raw linear progress (0.0 to 1.0) of the current transition animation, ignoring easing. Returns 0 when no transition is active. Use `getTransitionProgressEased` for the eased value.

functions.name: queueTransition
functions.description: Queue a transition to play automatically after the current one finishes. Multiple queued transitions execute in FIFO order, enabling multi-step cinematic sequences (e.g. fade-out then slide-in).

functions.name: getQueuedTransitionCount
functions.description: Returns the number of transitions waiting in the queue behind the currently-playing transition.

functions.name: clearQueuedTransitions
functions.description: Discard all queued transitions without affecting the currently-playing transition (if any). Use this to cancel a planned transition sequence mid-way.

functions.name: registerScene
functions.description: Register a scene table under a unique name for later retrieval via `getRegistered`, navigation via `popTo`, or deferred push via `pushPreloaded`. Registering does not push the scene onto the stack.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique name to associate with this scene (e.g. `"mainMenu"`, `"gameplay"`).

functions.name: getRegistered
functions.description: Retrieve a previously registered scene table by its name, or nil if no scene is registered under that name. Does not affect the stack.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The registered scene name to look up.

functions.name: hasRegistered
functions.description: Check whether a scene is registered under the given name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The scene name to look up.

functions.name: unregisterScene
functions.description: Remove a scene registration by name. Does not pop the scene if it is currently active on the stack — it only removes the name mapping.

functions.name: getRegisteredNames
functions.description: Returns an array of all currently registered scene name strings. Useful for debugging or building dynamic scene-selection UIs.

functions.name: setData
functions.description: Store an arbitrary Lua value in the scene module's shared data map, keyed by a string name. Scenes can use this to pass information between each other without direct references — for example, passing a selected level index from a menu scene to a gameplay scene.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key to store data under (e.g. `"selectedLevel"`, `"playerName"`).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to store (table, number, string, boolean, etc.). Overwrites any previous value for this key.

functions.name: getData
functions.description: Retrieve a value from the shared data map by key, or nil if the key has not been set. Commonly used in a scene's `enter` callback to read parameters set by the previous scene.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The data key to look up.

functions.name: hasData
functions.description: Check whether a key exists in the shared scene data map without retrieving its value.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The data key to check for.

functions.name: removeData
functions.description: Remove a key and its associated value from the shared scene data map. No-op if the key does not exist.

functions.name: newDepthSorter
functions.description: Create a new `LDepthSorter` instance for collecting drawable items and flushing them in depth-sorted (painter's algorithm) order. Allocate one per scene or per rendering pass.

functions.name: new
functions.description: Create a new scene instance from an optional prototype table. Sets up metatables so the instance inherits methods from the prototype. Use this for one-off scene creation; use `define` when you need a reusable scene constructor.

parameters.name: def
parameters.description: A prototype table containing scene lifecycle methods (`enter`, `leave`, `update`, `draw`, etc.). If omitted, an empty table is used.

functions.name: define
functions.description: Create a reusable scene constructor function from a prototype table. Each call to the returned factory produces a fresh instance that inherits methods from the prototype via metatables. Ideal for defining scene "classes" that can be instantiated multiple times.

parameters.name: def
parameters.description: A prototype table with scene lifecycle methods.

functions.name: getTransitionProgressEased
functions.description: Returns the eased progress (0.0 to 1.0) of the current transition, with the selected easing curve applied. Returns 0 when no transition is active. Use this instead of `getTransitionProgress` when you want smooth, non-linear animation values.

functions.name: pushOverlay
functions.description: Push a scene as a transparent overlay on top of the current scene. Unlike `push`, the underlying scene is NOT paused — it continues to receive `process`, `draw`, and `render` callbacks. Use overlays for pause menus, dialog boxes, inventory screens, or debug panels that should draw on top without stopping gameplay.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Transition animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Arbitrary data forwarded to the overlay's `enter(self, params)` callback.

functions.name: isOverlay
functions.description: Returns true if the current top scene was pushed via `pushOverlay`. Overlay scenes do not pause the scene beneath them, allowing both to update and render simultaneously.

functions.name: getActiveScenes
functions.description: Returns a Lua array of all active scene tables ordered by their layer value (lowest layer first). Includes both regular scenes and overlays. Useful for iterating over all scenes for custom processing or debugging.

functions.name: preload
functions.description: Register a deferred-loading function for a scene. The loader function is NOT called immediately — it runs the first time `pushPreloaded` is called with this name. Use this to spread scene initialization (asset loading, table setup) across loading screens or lazy-load heavy scenes on demand.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name to associate with the loader (must match the name used in `pushPreloaded`).

parameters.name: loader
parameters.description: A zero-argument function that creates and registers the scene via `registerScene` when called.

functions.name: isPreloaded
functions.description: Returns true if the named preload loader has already been executed at least once. Once a loader runs, subsequent `pushPreloaded` calls skip the loader and push the already-registered scene directly.

functions.name: pushPreloaded
functions.description: Push a preloaded scene onto the stack by name. If the loader registered via `preload` has not yet run, it executes first to create and register the scene. Then the registered scene is pushed with the specified transition. Combines deferred loading with stack navigation in a single call.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The preload/registration name (must match a prior `preload` or `registerScene` call).

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Transition animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Arbitrary data forwarded to the scene's `enter(self, params)` callback.

functions.name: getTransitionTypes
functions.description: Returns a Lua array of all supported transition type name strings. Use this to discover available transitions at runtime or build a transition picker UI.

functions.name: serializeScene
functions.description: Capture the current scene stack state as a serializable snapshot table. The snapshot contains a `stack` array of registered scene names (in stack order) and a `data` map of shared data key-value pairs. Use this for save/load systems to persist the player's navigation state.

functions.name: deserializeScene
functions.description: Restore shared scene data from a previously-serialized snapshot table. Only the `data` key-value map is restored; the scene stack itself must be rebuilt manually by pushing or registering scenes. Pair with `serializeScene` for save/load workflows.

parameters.name: snapshot
parameters.description: A snapshot table as returned by `serializeScene` (must contain a `data` field).

functions.name: fade
functions.description: Helper sub-table `lurek.scene.transitions` with convenience factory functions that build transition descriptor tables for use with transition-aware APIs.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Total fade duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.5.

functions.name: slide
functions.description: Create a directional slide transition descriptor table. The new scene slides in from the specified direction, pushing the old scene out.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Slide animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.4.

functions.name: wipe
functions.description: Create a horizontal wipe transition descriptor table. A wipe bar sweeps across the screen to reveal the new scene.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Wipe animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.5.

functions.name: iris
functions.description: Create an iris (circle) transition descriptor table. A circular aperture opens or closes to reveal the new scene, similar to classic cartoon transitions.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Iris animation duration in seconds. Defaults to 0.6.

modules.name: serial
modules.description: `lurek.serial` — Data serialization and deserialization with JSON, TOML, CSV, XML, INI, and MessagePack encoding/decoding for game configuration, save data, and inter-system data exchange.

functions.name: fromJson
functions.description: Parses a JSON string into a Lua table. Use this to load configuration files, network responses, or any structured data stored as JSON.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: A valid JSON string to parse.

functions.name: toJson
functions.description: Serializes a Lua value (table, string, number, boolean, or nil) into a JSON string. Useful for saving game state, writing config files, or preparing network payloads.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The Lua value to serialize into JSON.

parameters.name: pretty
parameters.description: When true, outputs indented human-readable JSON. Defaults to false (compact).

functions.name: fromToml
functions.description: Parses a TOML string into a Lua table. Ideal for loading game configuration files, level definitions, and engine settings stored in TOML format.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: A valid TOML string to parse.

functions.name: fromIni
functions.description: Parses an INI-format string into a Lua table. Sections become nested tables, and key-value pairs become string fields. Useful for legacy config files or simple settings.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: A valid INI string to parse.

functions.name: toToml
functions.description: Serializes a Lua table into a TOML-formatted string. Use this to write configuration files, save structured settings, or export data in a human-readable format.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The Lua table to serialize into TOML.

functions.name: fromCsv
functions.description: Parses a CSV string into a Lua table (array of rows). Each row is either a keyed table (when headers are present) or an indexed array of field values. Useful for loading spreadsheet exports, leaderboard data, or tabular game data.

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.description: Single-character field delimiter. Defaults to comma (",").

parameters.name: hasHeaders
parameters.description: When true, the first row is treated as column names and each data row becomes a keyed table. Defaults to true.

functions.name: toCsv
functions.description: Serializes a Lua table (array of row tables) into a CSV-formatted string. Each row table should have consistent keys or be an indexed array. Use this to export leaderboards, save tabular data, or generate spreadsheet-compatible output.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: An array of row tables to serialize.

parameters.name: delimiter
parameters.description: Single-character field delimiter. Defaults to comma (",").

parameters.name: hasHeaders
parameters.description: When true, writes column names as the first row. Defaults to true.

functions.name: encodeMsgPack
functions.description: Encodes a Lua table into a compact binary MessagePack string. MessagePack is faster and smaller than JSON, making it ideal for save files, network packets, or any scenario where performance matters more than human readability. The argument must be a table.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The Lua table to encode. Must be a table (not a primitive).

functions.name: decodeMsgPack
functions.description: Decodes a binary MessagePack string back into a Lua table. Use this to read save files, network packets, or any data previously encoded with encodeMsgPack.

parameters.name: bytes
parameters.description: A binary string containing valid MessagePack data.

functions.name: decodeXml
functions.description: Parses an XML string into a Lua table structure. Elements become nested tables with tag names as keys. Useful for loading Tiled map exports, SVG data, UI layout definitions, or other XML-based game assets.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: A valid XML string to parse.

functions.name: validate
functions.description: Validates a Lua value against a schema table. The schema defines expected types, required fields, and constraints. Returns a success boolean and an optional error message string describing the first validation failure. Use this to verify save data integrity or user-provided configuration before processing.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: A schema table defining the expected structure and constraints.

functions.name: detectFormat
functions.description: Attempts to auto-detect the serialization format of a string by inspecting its content (e.g., leading `{` for JSON, `[section]` for INI, XML declaration for XML). Returns the format name or nil if detection fails. Useful for loading user-provided files where the format is unknown.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The raw text content to analyze.

functions.name: decode
functions.description: Universal decoder that parses a string payload into a Lua table using the specified format. If no format is given, auto-detects from the content. Supports JSON, TOML, CSV, XML, INI, and MessagePack. Use this as a single entry point when handling files of varying or unknown formats.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: The raw string (or binary for msgpack) to decode.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format hint: "json", "toml", "csv", "xml", "ini", or "msgpack". Nil triggers auto-detection.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Optional settings table. For CSV: `delimiter` (string) and `has_headers` (boolean).

functions.name: encode
functions.description: Universal encoder that serializes a Lua value into the specified format. Supports JSON, TOML, CSV, and MessagePack. Returns a string (text for JSON/TOML/CSV, binary for MessagePack). Use this as a single entry point for all serialization needs.

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Optional settings table. For JSON: `pretty` (boolean). For CSV: `delimiter` (string) and `has_headers` (boolean).

functions.name: applyDefaults
functions.description: Merges a schema's default values into a data table, filling in any missing fields without overwriting existing ones. Use this to ensure game config or save data always has complete fields even when the user provides only partial overrides.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The data table that may have missing fields.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: A schema table containing `default` entries for fields.

modules.name: spine
modules.description: `lurek.spine` — Spine-like skeletal animation with bones, slots, attachments, IK constraints, and skin mixing.

functions.name: newSkeleton
functions.description: Creates a new empty skeleton with the given name. Add bones and slots to build the hierarchy.

functions.name: newSkeletonAnimation
functions.description: Creates a new empty animation with the given name and duration. Add keyframes to define motion.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name identifier for this animation (used with playAnimation).

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Total duration of the animation in seconds.

functions.name: animationFromJson
functions.description: Parses a JSON string into a SkeletonAnimation. Returns nil if parsing fails or the format is invalid.

parameters.name: json
parameters.description: JSON string describing the animation (Spine-compatible format).

modules.name: sprite
modules.description: `lurek.sprite` - Provides sprite batch rendering, sprite sheets, quad management, and texture atlas operations for efficient 2D rendering.

functions.name: newSheet
functions.description: Creates a new sprite sheet by dividing a texture of the given pixel size into a grid of equal-sized frames.

functions.name: newRPGMakerSheet
functions.description: Creates a sprite sheet using RPG Maker's standard character layout (4 columns × 4 rows per character block).

functions.name: parseAtlas
functions.description: Parses a TexturePacker JSON atlas string and returns a sprite atlas object.

parameters.name: json_str
parameters.description: Raw JSON content of the TexturePacker atlas file.

functions.name: newAtlasSheet
functions.description: Creates a sprite sheet from an existing atlas, treating each atlas entry as a frame within the given sheet dimensions.

functions.name: parseAsepriteAtlas
functions.description: Parses an Aseprite JSON atlas string and returns a sprite atlas object.

parameters.name: json_str
parameters.description: Raw JSON content of the Aseprite export atlas file.

modules.name: system
modules.description: `lurek.system` - Provides OS-level utilities including clipboard, system info, environment variables, and platform detection.

functions.name: getOS
functions.description: Returns the name of the host operating system as a string.

functions.name: getVersion
functions.description: Returns the semantic version string of the Lurek2D engine.

functions.name: getProcessorCount
functions.description: Returns the number of logical processors available on the host machine.

functions.name: getMemorySize
functions.description: Returns the total physical memory of the host system in megabytes.

functions.name: openURL
functions.description: Opens a URL in the default system browser. Only `http://`, `https://`, and `mailto:` schemes are permitted.

functions.name: getPreferredLocales
functions.description: Returns a list of the user's preferred locale identifiers from the operating system.

functions.name: getPowerInfo
functions.description: Returns the current power supply state, battery percentage, and estimated time remaining.

functions.name: getInfo
functions.description: Returns a table with comprehensive engine and host information.

functions.name: getMessage
functions.description: Resolves a message string by its identifier from the engine message catalog.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The message identifier to look up.

functions.name: hasMessage
functions.description: Checks whether a message identifier exists in the engine message catalog.

functions.name: getMessageCount
functions.description: Returns the total number of messages registered in the engine message catalog.

functions.name: setClipboardText
functions.description: Copies a string to the system clipboard. Logs a warning if the clipboard is unavailable or the write fails.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to place on the clipboard.

functions.name: getClipboardText
functions.description: Reads the current text content from the system clipboard. Returns an empty string if the clipboard is unavailable or contains no text.

functions.name: reloadConfig
functions.description: Requests a reload of the engine configuration from `conf.lua`. The reload is deferred until the next frame.

functions.name: getConfig
functions.description: Returns a table containing the current engine runtime configuration values.

functions.name: setDebugOverlay
functions.description: Enables or disables the on-screen debug overlay that shows FPS, draw calls, and other diagnostics.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: `true` to show the debug overlay, `false` to hide it.

functions.name: getDebugOverlay
functions.description: Returns whether the on-screen debug overlay is currently enabled.

functions.name: setLogLevel
functions.description: Sets the engine-wide log verbosity level at runtime.

functions.name: getLogLevel
functions.description: Returns the current engine log verbosity level as a string.

functions.name: log
functions.description: Writes a message to the engine log at the specified severity level.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Log level: `"error"`, `"warn"`, `"info"`, `"debug"`, or `"trace"`. Defaults to `"info"` if unrecognized.

functions.name: getLastError
functions.description: Returns the last error for Lua scripts in this module.

functions.name: errorSnapshot
functions.description: Creates a JSON-encoded error snapshot from a message string, useful for diagnostics and error reporting.

functions.name: getArch
functions.description: Returns the CPU architecture of the host system.

functions.name: getEnv
functions.description: Reads an environment variable by name. Returns `nil` if the variable is not set.

functions.name: getArgs
functions.description: Returns the command-line arguments passed to the engine as a 1-indexed table of strings.

functions.name: parseArgs
functions.description: Parses command-line arguments into structured flags, options, and positional values. Supports `--key=value`, `--key value`, `-flag`, and `--` end-of-options.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Optional table of argument strings. Uses `os.args` if omitted.

functions.name: runBatch
functions.description: Executes a table of named task functions sequentially, collecting pass/fail results and elapsed time for each.

parameters.name: tasks
parameters.description: Table mapping task names (string) to task functions (function).

parameters.name: opts
parameters.description: Options table. Set `stopOnError = true` to skip remaining tasks after the first failure.

functions.name: getBatchResults
functions.description: Summarizes batch results by counting passed, failed, and skipped tasks.

modules.name: terminal
modules.description: `lurek.terminal` - Provides an in-game terminal emulator with command parsing, history, output buffering, and ANSI-style formatting.

functions.name: newTerminal
functions.description: Creates a new terminal emulator grid with the given column and row count.

functions.name: newLabel
functions.description: Creates a new label widget that displays static text at the given cell position.

functions.name: newButton
functions.description: Creates a new clickable button widget with the given position, size, and label text.

functions.name: newTextBox
functions.description: Creates a new single-line text input widget at the given position with a fixed width.

functions.name: newList
functions.description: Creates a new scrollable list widget for displaying and selecting items.

functions.name: newBorder
functions.description: Creates a new decorative border widget drawn using box-drawing characters.

functions.name: newPanel
functions.description: Creates a new panel widget that can contain child widgets for grouped layout.

functions.name: pushScrollback
functions.description: Appends a line of text to the terminal scrollback buffer for later retrieval.

functions.name: getScrollback
functions.description: Retrieves a range of lines from the terminal scrollback buffer.

functions.name: scrollbackLen
functions.description: Returns the number of lines currently stored in the terminal scrollback buffer.

functions.name: setScrollbackCap
functions.description: Sets the maximum number of lines retained in the terminal scrollback buffer. Older lines are discarded when the cap is exceeded.

functions.name: pushCmdHistory
functions.description: Appends a command string to the terminal command history for up/down arrow recall.

functions.name: prevCmd
functions.description: Navigates backward in the terminal command history, returning the previous command or nil if at the start.

functions.name: nextCmd
functions.description: Navigates forward in the terminal command history, returning the next command or nil if at the end.

functions.name: cmdHistoryLen
functions.description: Returns the number of commands currently stored in the terminal command history.

functions.name: clearCmdHistory
functions.description: Removes all entries from the terminal command history.

functions.name: applyTheme
functions.description: Applies a named color theme to the terminal, setting default foreground and background colors.

functions.name: printHighlighted
functions.description: Renders syntax-highlighted text onto the terminal grid using a table of highlight rules with regex patterns and colors.

parameters.name: rules
parameters.description: Array of rule tables, each with `pattern` (string), `fg` (table {r,g,b} 0-255), and optional `bg` (table {r,g,b} 0-255).

functions.name: stripAnsi
functions.description: Removes all ANSI escape sequences from a string, returning plain text.

functions.name: parseAnsi
functions.description: Parses ANSI escape sequences in a string into an array of span tables with text, bold, fg, and bg fields.

functions.name: printAnsi
functions.description: Renders ANSI-colored text directly onto the terminal grid at the given cell position.

functions.name: addCompletion
functions.description: Registers a candidate string for tab-completion in the shared completion engine.

functions.name: removeCompletion
functions.description: Removes a previously registered completion candidate from the shared completion engine.

functions.name: clearCompletions
functions.description: Removes all registered completion candidates from the shared completion engine.

functions.name: getCompletions
functions.description: Returns all completion candidates matching the given prefix string.

functions.name: nextCompletion
functions.description: Cycles to the next matching completion candidate for the given prefix, wrapping around after the last match.

functions.name: resetCompletion
functions.description: Resets the completion cycling state so the next call to nextCompletion starts from the first match.

functions.name: getMaxCols
functions.description: Returns the engine-defined maximum number of columns a terminal grid can have.

functions.name: getMaxRows
functions.description: Returns the engine-defined maximum number of rows a terminal grid can have.

modules.name: thread
modules.description: `lurek.thread` - Provides multi-threaded Lua worker VMs with typed channel messaging for parallel game logic execution.

functions.name: newThread
functions.description: Creates a new worker thread that will execute the given Lua code string when started.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Lua source code to run in the worker VM.

functions.name: newChannel
functions.description: Creates a new unbounded channel for sending typed values between threads.

functions.name: newBoundedChannel
functions.description: Creates a new bounded channel with a fixed capacity, blocking pushes when full.

parameters.name: capacity
parameters.description: Maximum number of items the channel can hold.

functions.name: getChannel
functions.description: Returns a named shared channel, creating it on first access. Repeated calls with the same name return the same channel.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique name identifying the shared channel.

functions.name: newPool
functions.description: Creates a fixed-size thread pool where each worker runs the same Lua code and consumes items from a shared input channel.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of worker threads to spawn.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Lua source code each worker thread will execute.

functions.name: async
functions.description: Runs a Lua code string or dumped function asynchronously on a new worker thread, returning a promise for the result.

parameters.name: codeOrFunc
parameters.description: Lua source code or a dumpable Lua function to execute.
Additional arguments forwarded to the worker.

functions.name: getWorkerCapabilities
functions.description: Returns a list of capability names available inside worker VMs (e.g. which `lurek.*` modules are accessible).

modules.name: tilemap
modules.description: `lurek.tilemap` - Provides tile-based map rendering with layers, animated tiles, auto-tiling, collision maps, and TMX/Tiled import.

functions.name: newTileSet
functions.description: Creates a new tileset from atlas parameters.

parameters.name: margin
parameters.description: Pixel margin around the atlas edge (default 0).

functions.name: newTileMap
functions.description: Creates a new empty tilemap with the given tile dimensions.

functions.name: newAutoTileSheet
functions.description: Creates an auto-tile sheet with a given tile size and layout.

functions.name: newChunkMap
functions.description: Creates a new infinite chunk-based tile map.

functions.name: newIsoMap
functions.description: Creates a new isometric map with the given dimensions and tile geometry.

parameters.name: partCount
parameters.description: Number of tile parts per cell (default 4).

functions.name: newMapBlock
functions.description: Creates a new procedural map block with the given dimensions.

functions.name: newMapGroup
functions.description: Creates a new map group to hold blocks and generation scripts.

functions.name: toScreenIso
functions.description: Converts tile coordinates to screen-space position for isometric projection.

functions.name: fromScreenIso
functions.description: Converts screen-space coordinates back to tile coordinates for isometric projection.

functions.name: toScreenHex
functions.description: Converts axial hex coordinates to screen-space pixel position.

functions.name: fromScreenHex
functions.description: Converts screen-space pixel coordinates to axial hex coordinates.

functions.name: hexNeighbors
functions.description: Returns the six neighboring hex cells of a given axial coordinate.

functions.name: hexDistance
functions.description: Computes the hex grid distance between two axial coordinates.

functions.name: hexRound
functions.description: Rounds fractional axial hex coordinates to the nearest integer hex cell.

functions.name: hexLine
functions.description: Returns all hex cells along a line between two axial coordinates.

functions.name: hexRing
functions.description: Returns all hex cells forming a ring at a given radius around a center.

functions.name: hexSpiral
functions.description: Returns all hex cells in a spiral pattern out to a given radius.

functions.name: hexArea
functions.description: Returns all hex cells within a filled area of a given radius.

functions.name: hexRotate
functions.description: Rotates a hex cell around a center point by a number of 60-degree steps.

functions.name: hexReflect
functions.description: Reflects a hex cell across an axis through a center point.

functions.name: isoRotate
functions.description: Rotates an isometric direction index by a number of 90-degree steps.

functions.name: isoDirectionName
functions.description: Returns a human-readable name for an isometric direction index.

functions.name: isoDirectionFromAngle
functions.description: Converts an angle in degrees to the nearest isometric direction index.

functions.name: newMapScript
functions.description: Creates a new empty map-generation script.

functions.name: newMapGen
